stock_a1200.label: Amiga 1200 (stock)
stock_a1200.notes: Base A1200 with 2MB Chip RAM only. Limited stack headroom.

a1200_accel.label: Amiga 1200 + Accelerator
a1200_accel.notes: A1200 with 68030 accelerator (e.g. Blizzard 1230) and 8MB Fast RAM

candidates.name: basename
candidates.description: Strip directory and suffix from filenames
candidates.notes: Trivial single-file utility, no POSIX deps beyond stdio

candidates.name: dirname
candidates.notes: Trivial single-file utility

candidates.name: tr
candidates.notes: Multi-file but pure stdio; locale/wchar paths need #ifdef __AMIGA__

candidates.name: fmt
candidates.notes: Pure text processing, no OS deps

candidates.name: fold
candidates.notes: Single file, pure stdio

candidates.name: rev
candidates.notes: Trivial single-file, ~50 lines

candidates.name: column
candidates.notes: Uses ioctl for terminal width -- stub to 80 columns

candidates.name: expand
candidates.notes: Single file, pure stdio

candidates.name: unexpand
candidates.notes: Single file, pure stdio

candidates.name: paste
candidates.description: Merge lines of files side by side
candidates.notes: Single file, pure stdio

candidates.name: join
candidates.description: Relational database join on sorted files
candidates.notes: Single file, locale-aware sorting needs review

candidates.name: comm
candidates.description: Select or reject lines common to two files
candidates.notes: Single file, pure stdio

candidates.name: tsort
candidates.notes: Single file, pure stdio + malloc

candidates.name: ls
candidates.notes: Multi-file; needs stat/readdir shims, ioctl for columns, no user/group on Amiga

candidates.name: file
candidates.description: Determine file type via magic numbers
candidates.notes: Needs magic database file; bundled magic.mgc or simplified rules

candidates.name: du
candidates.notes: Uses stat/opendir/readdir; block size semantics differ on Amiga

candidates.name: find
candidates.notes: Needs fork/exec for -exec flag -- stub or omit -exec; rest is feasible

candidates.name: xargs
candidates.description: Build and execute command lines from stdin
candidates.notes: xargs without -P is feasible; uses SystemTagList for execution

candidates.name: touch
candidates.description: Change file access and modification times
candidates.notes: Uses utimes/stat; SetFileDate on AmigaOS, epoch offset needed

candidates.name: ln
candidates.notes: Amiga has soft links via MakeLink; hard links not available

candidates.name: cp
candidates.notes: Multi-file; -r needs opendir/readdir, no chmod/chown semantics

candidates.name: mv
candidates.notes: Uses rename(); cross-device needs copy+delete fallback

candidates.name: rm
candidates.notes: Uses unlink/rmdir shims; -r needs recursive opendir walk

candidates.name: mkdir
candidates.notes: Simple; -p needs recursive creation; chmod mode bits are no-ops

candidates.name: rmdir
candidates.notes: Trivial; uses rmdir shim

candidates.name: chmod
candidates.notes: Maps to SetProtection; only r/w/e bits, no user/group/other

candidates.name: env
candidates.notes: Print mode trivial; exec mode needs SystemTagList

candidates.name: printenv
candidates.description: Print all or part of environment
candidates.notes: Needs environ enumeration -- scan ENV: on Amiga

candidates.name: uname
candidates.notes: Stub uname() to return AmigaOS/68k info from exec.library

candidates.name: hostname
candidates.notes: Use GetVar HOSTNAME or bsdsocket.library gethostname()

candidates.name: id
candidates.notes: Amiga has no /etc/passwd -- stub getpwnam, return uid=0

candidates.name: whoami
candidates.notes: Amiga has no /etc/passwd -- stub getpwnam, return 'user'

candidates.name: logname
candidates.notes: Trivial; stub getlogin() to return 'user'

candidates.name: tty
candidates.description: Print name of terminal on stdin
candidates.notes: Stub ttyname() to return 'CON:'; isatty via IsInteractive

candidates.name: echo
candidates.notes: Trivial ~30 lines; no POSIX deps

candidates.name: printf
candidates.notes: Single file; implements printf format parsing in C

candidates.name: nl
candidates.notes: Uses regex for section delimiters; regex shim available

candidates.name: colrm
candidates.notes: Single file, pure stdio

candidates.name: cat
candidates.notes: Simple; raw mode (-u) can use read/write shims for unbuffered I/O

candidates.name: true
candidates.notes: Trivial; one line of C

candidates.name: false
candidates.notes: Trivial; exit(RETURN_ERROR) for Amiga shell compatibility

candidates.name: sleep
candidates.notes: Trivial; uses amiport_sleep shim

analysis.blocker_workaround: nanosleep can be replaced with amiport_sleep() for whole seconds or Delay() for sub-second; getprogname replaced with __progname

candidates.name: date
candidates.description: Display or set date and time
candidates.notes: Needs epoch offset (1978 vs 1970); strftime from libnix; set mode stubs

candidates.name: expr
candidates.notes: Uses regex for : operator; regex shim available

candidates.name: test
candidates.notes: Uses stat for file tests; stub user/group tests

candidates.name: seq
candidates.notes: OpenBSD has jot not seq; sbase seq is simpler alternative

candidates.name: jot
candidates.notes: Uses arc4random -- replace with rand(); single file

candidates.name: factor
candidates.description: Factor a number and print prime factors
candidates.notes: Pure math, no POSIX deps; may use unsigned long long

candidates.name: which
candidates.description: Locate a program file in the user's path
candidates.notes: Scan C: directory on Amiga instead of PATH

candidates.name: nohup
candidates.notes: Needs exec -- use SystemTagList; no SIGHUP on Amiga

candidates.name: look
candidates.description: Display lines beginning with a given string
candidates.notes: Binary search on sorted file; needs /usr/share/dict/words substitute

candidates.name: strings
candidates.notes: Single file, pure stdio; useful for Amiga hunk binaries

candidates.name: cmp
candidates.description: Compare two files byte by byte
candidates.notes: Multi-file but simple; uses fgetc/fread

candidates.name: cksum
candidates.description: Display file checksums and block counts
candidates.notes: CRC32/sum algorithms are pure C; may include MD5/SHA if bundled

candidates.name: md5
candidates.notes: Pure C hash implementation; also handles sha256/sha512 modes

candidates.name: pr
candidates.notes: Multi-column pagination; locale/time functions needed

candidates.name: col
candidates.description: Filter reverse line feeds from input
candidates.notes: Single file, pure character processing

candidates.name: vis
candidates.notes: Uses vis(3) library function -- may need to bundle

candidates.name: rs
candidates.notes: Matrix reshape; pure stdio + malloc

candidates.name: dc
candidates.notes: Arbitrary precision; companion to bc; pure C

candidates.name: apply
candidates.notes: Uses system() -- maps to SystemTagList on Amiga

candidates.name: awk
candidates.notes: Plan 9 awk is one true awk (BWK); simpler than gawk, no fork needed

candidates.name: getopt
candidates.notes: Trivial wrapper around getopt(3); useful for shell scripts

candidates.name: seq
candidates.notes: Single-file sbase implementation; simpler than OpenBSD jot

candidates.name: tac
candidates.description: Concatenate files in reverse line order
candidates.notes: Single-file reverse cat; uses malloc + lseek

candidates.name: sponge
candidates.description: Soak up stdin and write to file
candidates.notes: Single file; reads all stdin then writes to file; uses tmpfile

candidates.name: install
candidates.notes: File copy with mode bits; chmod is a no-op but copy is useful

candidates.name: unicode
candidates.notes: UTF-8/codepoint lookup; pure C, no OS deps

candidates.name: freq
candidates.notes: Byte frequency counter; trivial single-file

candidates.name: ascii
candidates.notes: ASCII table display; trivial, no OS deps

candidates.name: hoc
candidates.notes: K&R calculator; uses yacc-generated parser, may need FPU emulation

candidates.name: uuencode
candidates.notes: Single file; pure stdio encoding

candidates.name: uudecode
candidates.notes: Single file; companion to uuencode

candidates.name: base64
candidates.notes: Single file; pure encoding algorithm

candidates.name: sha256sum
candidates.notes: Pure C hash; single file with bundled sha256

candidates.name: md5sum
candidates.notes: Pure C hash; single file with bundled md5

candidates.name: xd
candidates.notes: Hex/octal dump; single file, pure stdio

candidates.name: split
candidates.notes: Single file; uses stdio for reading and writing chunks

candidates.name: od
candidates.notes: Octal dump with multiple formats; single file

candidates.name: sha1sum
candidates.notes: Pure C hash; single file with bundled sha1

ported.name: tail
ported.description: Display last part of a file

ported.name: patch
ported.description: Apply diff patches to source files

ported.name: less
ported.description: Terminal pager with search and scroll
